description: Document auto-injected tool parameters (`tabId` and `instance`) in docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx. The platform injects these into every plugin tool's input schema at MCP registration time: `tabId` (optional integer) is injected into every plugin tool, and `instance` (required string enum) is injected whenever a plugin has 2+ instances configured via `configSchema` url-type settings. AI agents connecting to /mcp see the modified schemas but the reference doc doesn't explain where these parameters come from, when they are required, or how to discover valid values — particularly critical for multi-instance plugins where `instance` is REQUIRED and the call will fail without it.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add 'Auto-injected parameters' section to MCP Server reference
userStories.description: Add a new subsection under `## /mcp — MCP Streamable HTTP` in docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx that documents the two parameters the platform injects into every plugin tool's input schema: optional `tabId: integer` (present on all plugin tools) and required `instance: enum` (present only when a plugin has 2+ configured instances via a url-type configSchema field). Reference the same injection for gateway MCP and the REST /tools endpoints. Explain how agents discover valid tabIds and instance names via `plugin_list_tabs`.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx contains a new subsection under '## /mcp — MCP Streamable HTTP' (or a top-level sibling section like '## Auto-injected tool parameters') that explicitly documents `tabId` parameter injection on plugin tools
The same section documents `instance` parameter injection when a plugin has 2+ configured instances (Object.keys(plugin.instanceMap).length >= 2)
The docs clearly state `instance` is REQUIRED when present — calls without it fail validation
The `instance` values are an enum derived from the plugin's instanceMap keys (user-provided instance names from config.json settings)
The documentation states that auto-injection applies equally to /mcp, /mcp/gateway (via opentabs_call), and the REST /tools/:name/call endpoint — same schemas across all three
Cross-link to `plugin_list_tabs` as the way for agents to discover valid tabIds and per-tab `instance` labels
Cross-link to /docs/reference/configuration#settings for how users configure multi-instance url settings
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (add new subsection, update frontmatter)

Context:
The MCP setup code in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (specifically `getAllToolsList`, around lines 344-402) does a `structuredClone` of each plugin tool's input_schema and then injects:

1. `tabId` — always injected. Schema: `{ type: 'integer', minimum: 1, description: 'Optional. Target a specific browser tab by its ID. When omitted, the platform automatically selects the best matching tab. Use browser_list_tabs or plugin_list_tabs to discover tab IDs.' }`

2. `instance` — injected only when `Object.keys(plugin.instanceMap).length >= 2`. Schema: `{ type: 'string', enum: instanceNames, description: 'Target a named instance. Required when multiple instances are configured. Valid values: <list>.' }` and `instance` is pushed onto the schema's `required` array.

The `instanceMap` itself comes from `resolvePluginSettings` (platform/mcp-server/src/settings-resolver.ts) — a map from user-chosen instance name → Chrome match pattern, derived from the user's `Record<string, string>` value of each `url`-type configSchema field. Example: if a user sets `settings.jira.instanceUrl = { "work": "https://acme.atlassian.net", "personal": "https://me.atlassian.net" }`, the plugin gets `instanceMap = { work: '*://acme.atlassian.net/*', personal: '*://me.atlassian.net/*' }` and `instance` becomes a required enum of `['work', 'personal']`.

The same injection applies uniformly to:
- /mcp (full MCP) via getAllToolsList
- /mcp/gateway (meta-tools' view of the tools list)
- REST /tools endpoint (GET /tools) and REST /tools/:name/call

Agents discover valid tabIds AND valid instance labels via `plugin_list_tabs` (see platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/plugin-list-tabs.ts). Each returned tab includes an optional `instance` field derived from matching its URL against the plugin's `instanceMap` entries.

The existing mcp-server.mdx already has a `### MCP Capabilities` subsection under `## /mcp — MCP Streamable HTTP` (around lines 239-246). The new content should go near that section to preserve topical flow.

Changes:
1. In docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx, add a new level-3 section `### Auto-injected tool parameters` immediately after `### MCP Capabilities` (before the next `---` horizontal rule).
2. The section should explain:
   - The platform injects `tabId` (optional `integer`, minimum 1) into every plugin tool's input schema at registration time. This is transparent to plugin authors — the tool's own Zod input schema does not declare it.
   - When a plugin has two or more instances configured (via url-type configSchema fields whose user value is a `Record<string, string>` map), the platform additionally injects `instance` (required string enum). The enum values are the user-chosen instance names. Omitting `instance` causes the MCP call to fail schema validation.
   - These injected parameters apply uniformly to /mcp, /mcp/gateway (the underlying opentabs_call), and the REST endpoints (GET /tools, POST /tools/:name/call) — all three see the same schemas.
   - Agents can discover valid `tabId` values via `plugin_list_tabs`. The same tool returns an `instance` label per tab when multi-instance is active.
3. Use a small <Table> or an ordered list to compare the two injected parameters in a structured way, showing: name, type, required?, source.
4. Include at least one JSON example showing a tools/call payload for a multi-instance plugin that passes `instance` and `tabId`, e.g.:
   ```json
   {
     "name": "jira_list_issues",
     "arguments": {
       "instance": "work",
       "tabId": 42,
       "project": "PLAT"
     }
   }
   ```
5. Cross-link to:
   - The plugin_list_tabs description in /docs/reference/browser-tools#site-analysis (or wherever it is described).
   - /docs/reference/configuration#settings for how users configure url-type settings with multiple instance entries.
6. Update the frontmatter `lastUpdated: 2026-04-15` at the top of the file to `lastUpdated: 2026-04-24`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the behavior of any source code — this is a docs-only change.
- Do NOT rewrite or merge existing sections (like `## Connection Modes`, `### MCP Capabilities`, the endpoints table, `## /health`, etc.) — only add the new subsection.
- Do NOT claim that plugins can ADD `tabId` or `instance` to their own Zod schemas — these are platform-level injections. Plugin tool handlers never see them in their input; the platform strips them before the handler runs. The docs already hint at this for `tabId` in the /docs/reference/browser-tools site-analysis row; keep the same stance.
- Do NOT conflate `tabId` (a browser.tabs integer) with `connectionId` (a browser profile identifier). The injected parameter is `tabId` only.
- Do NOT describe `instance` as injected when only 1 instance is configured — the platform injects it only when Object.keys(instanceMap).length >= 2. If a plugin has 0 or 1 instance configured, the field is not present on the tool's input schema.
- Match the existing MDX style — use <Table> for structured tables, plain fenced code blocks for JSON examples, and the Table.Header/Table.Row/Table.Cell pattern that the rest of the file uses.
